toolCalls.argumentsJson: {"observations": [{"text": "User explicitly requested title 'Validate local Gemma fallback', P1 priority, due 2026-07-04, and 2.5h estimate in the 2026-06-21 09:00 log entry. All four metadata fields were unset, so I applied them directly.", "priority": "routine", "category": "operational"}, {"text": "User is skeptical of shallow tool-call smoke reports and wants a real app-shaped local eval. This means the checklist item 'Run a meaningful local app eval' should be treated as the core deliverable — not just a quick prompt-and-check, but something that exercises the actual Lotti task-agent workflow (metadata tools, report generation, etc.). The Qwen baseline task (task-qwen-baseline) already passed app-shaped checks, so Gemma should be evaluated against the same bar.", "priority": "notable", "category": "operational"}]}

toolCalls.name: update_report
toolCalls.argumentsJson:
{"oneLiner": "Validating whether local Gemma can serve as a Lotti task-agent fallback", "tldr": "New task to evaluate the downloaded local Gemma oMLX model against Lotti's task-agent workflow. Set to P1, due July 4, estimated 2.5h. Two checklist items pending: a meaningful app-shaped eval and a head-to-head comparison with Qwen.", "content": "## 📌 What is left to do

- [ ] Run a meaningful local app eval
- [ ] Compare Gemma against Qwen on task-agent behavior

## Context

The parent project — **Local inference reliability** — notes that Qwen is the current local default and Gemma needs stronger validation before it can be trusted. The linked [Qwen 3.6 local baseline](/tasks/task-qwen-baseline) task already confirms Qwen can emit task metadata tools and a final report through oMLX, giving us a concrete comparison bar.

The user wants a **real app-shaped local eval**, not a shallow tool-call smoke test. That means exercising the actual Lotti task-agent workflow — metadata tool calls, report generation, and behavioral quality — the same way Qwen was validated.

## ⚠️ Risks

- **Shallow eval trap:** The user is explicitly skeptical of surface-level smoke reports. The eval must exercise the full task-agent loop, not just a single prompt/response check.
- **Comparison gap:** Without running Gemma through the same app-shaped checks Qwen passed, any "it works" conclusion will be unconvincing.

## 💡 Learnings

- Qwen baseline is the reference point — Gemma should be evaluated against the same app-shaped checks that Qwen already passed.
- Focus on runtime behavior that affects the Lotti task-agent workflow, not generic benchmark scores."}

toolCalls.argumentsJson: {"items": [{"title": "Mit Ben den API-Umfang klären"}, {"title": "Figma-Prototyp fertigstellen"}, {"title": "Anmeldung implementieren"}, {"title": "Lea um Security-Review bitten"}]}

toolCalls.name: record_observations
toolCalls.argumentsJson: {"observations": [{"text": "First wake for task-client-portal. User left a voice note on 2026-07-10 requesting concrete checklist items derived from their described Beta preparation steps. Created four checklist items: API scope clarification with Ben, Figma prototype completion, login implementation, and security review request to Lea. The sequence follows the order the user mentioned, which implies a dependency chain (API scope → prototype → login → security review).", "priority": "routine", "category": "operational"}]}

toolCalls.name: update_report
toolCalls.argumentsJson:
{"oneLiner": "Beta-Vorbereitung: 4 Checklisteneinträge erstellt, API-Umfang mit Ben als erster Schritt", "tldr": "Kundenportal-Beta bis 30. September. Vier konkrete Checklisteneinträge aus der Sprachnotiz erstellt — von API-Klärung bis Security-Review. 🚀", "content": "## 📋 Status

Das Kundenportal wird auf die Beta-Veröffentlichung am **30. September 2026** vorbereitet. Aus der Sprachnotiz vom 10. Juli wurden vier konkrete Checklisteneinträge abgeleitet.

## 📌 Was noch zu tun ist

- [ ] Mit Ben den API-Umfang klären
- [ ] Figma-Prototyp fertigstellen
- [ ] Anmeldung implementieren
- [ ] Lea um Security-Review bitten

Die Reihenfolge folgt der genannten Abhängigkeitskette: Zunächst muss der API-Umfang mit Ben geklärt werden, bevor der Figma-Prototyp finalisiert werden kann. Danach folgt die Implementierung der Anmeldung, und abschließend bittet Lea um den Security-Review.

## 💡 Learnings

- Die Beta-Vorbereitung hat eine klare Abhängigkeitskette: API-Umfang → Prototyp → Anmeldung → Security-Review.
- Beteiligte Personen: **Ben** (API), **Lea** (Security-Review).
"}

Legal review remains pending — blocked on Dana confirming the retention clause. Moved due date from 2026-09-30 to 2026-10-15 per explicit user instruction in the log entry.", "priority": "routine", "category": "operational"}]}

{"oneLiner": "Customer interviews done; legal review blocked on Dana's retention clause, deadline moved to Oct 15", "tldr": "All five customer interviews are complete ✅. Legal review is pending and blocked while Dana confirms the retention clause. Launch deadline moved to October 15, 2026.", "content": "## ✅ Achieved

- All five customer interviews completed.

## 📌 What is left to do

- [ ] Complete legal review — blocked on Dana confirming the retention clause.

## ⚠️ Blockers

- **Legal review** is on hold pending Dana's confirmation of the retention clause. This must be resolved before the portal can launch.

## 💡 Learnings

- The launch deadline was moved from September 30 to **October 15, 2026** to accommodate the legal review delay."}

{"oneLiner": "Duplicate sync events resurfaced in QA — investigation needed", "tldr": "## Achieved
- Fixed duplicate sync events (initial fix)

## ⚠️ Renewed Risk
QA reports that duplicate sync events reappeared once after reconnecting two devices. No root cause has been identified yet. The initial fix may be incomplete or conditional on specific reconnection scenarios.

## 📌 What is left to do
- [ ] Investigate root cause of duplicate sync events resurfacing on device reconnection
- [ ] Validate fix across multi-device reconnection scenarios

## 💡 Learnings
- The duplicate-event fix appears to hold in normal operation but fails under multi-device reconnection conditions. QA testing should explicitly cover reconnection flows going forward.
"}
